vulnerabilities.description: Resubmit same message with different message_id
vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: UsedMessages storage map + derive_message_id function
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Two-layer replay protection: (1) UsedMessages checks if message_id already processed, (2) UsedNonces prevents resubmission under new message_id
vulnerabilities.impact: Could execute same transfer twice, inflating balances
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Intercept message, re-derive with same content but modified message_id → system would process twice
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - replay protection correctly prevents this via UsedMessages[message_id] set lookup
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: COMPREHENSIVE - bootstrap_x3_asset() confirms single-pass execution
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: UsedMessages storage guarantees O(1) idempotency check

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: NextNonce double map (DomainId, AccountBytes) → Nonce
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Per-(source_domain, sender, nonce) dedup enforced at router layer
vulnerabilities.impact: Resubmit same extrinsic multiple times
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Send xvm_transfer with nonce=5, wait for on-chain confirmation, send again with nonce=5
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - DuplicateNonce error blocks reuse
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: COMPREHENSIVE - nonce semantics verified in test fixtures
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: NextNonce strictly monotonic with DuplicateNonce enforcement

vulnerabilities.description: Source debited but destination credit fails
vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: do_initiate_transfer + do_complete_transfer state machine
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: State transitions: SourceDebited → DestinationCredited → Finalized enforced via TransferStatus::can_transition_to
vulnerabilities.impact: Funds locked in SourceDebited state indefinitely
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Initiate transfer (SourceDebited), then DoS completion call to prevent DestinationCredited
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - Expiry mechanism via cancel_expired_xvm_transfer enables refund after expires_at
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: COMPREHENSIVE - all state transitions tested
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Expiry + refund mechanism drives transfer back to source

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: comment '4. Calls the ledger: debit source → pending.' + 'King invariant'
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Every ledger mutation is single transactional call; rolls back entire extrinsic if invariant breaks
vulnerabilities.impact: If destination credit fails, source debit also rolls back
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Craft transaction that passes validation but fails during ledger write
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - transactional semantics prevent partial state corruption
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: COMPREHENSIVE - test_x3_native_evm_svm_roundtrip_preserves_supply validates invariant
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Atomic ledger writes with rollback guarantee

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: xvm_transfer validation + 'BadExpiry' error
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Code comment: 'Expiry block is not in the future' - validates expires_at > current_block
vulnerabilities.impact: Transfer immediately refundable without completing
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Call xvm_transfer(expires_at: 0), then immediately cancel_expired_xvm_transfer
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - BadExpiry check prevents past/current block
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: PARTIAL - error handling tested but not shown in excerpt
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: BadExpiry validation at transfer initiation

vulnerabilities.description: Set expires_at to far future (lock funds indefinitely)
vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: no upper bound check on expires_at
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Pallet accepts any future block number without sanity cap
vulnerabilities.impact: Funds can be locked for arbitrary duration
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Set expires_at to u32::MAX (4 billion blocks ≈ 8000+ years)
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: FOUND - No MaxExpiryBlocks parameter visible in code excerpt
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: NOT VISIBLE - no test showing upper bound enforcement
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Expiry mechanism enables cancellation, but no upper bound prevents abuse

vulnerabilities.description: Reuse old nonce from different sender
vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: NextNonce is DoubleMap with (DomainId, AccountBytes) keys
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Nonce tracking is per-(domain, sender) pair, so Alice's nonce=5 and Bob's nonce=5 are separate
vulnerabilities.impact: No cross-user nonce contamination
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: N/A - nonce scoping prevents reuse across senders
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - proper nonce isolation
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: IMPLIED - nonce fixtures (alice_native, alice_evm, alice_svm) show multi-user support
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: DoubleMap keying prevents cross-user nonce collision

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: Nonce type not shown but likely u64 or u32
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: No visible overflow check in excerpt
vulnerabilities.impact: After 2^64 transfers, nonce wraps and allows replay
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Execute 2^64 transfers per sender, then submit old transfer under wrapped nonce
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: UNKNOWN - type definition not in excerpt
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: NOT VISIBLE - no overflow test
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Practical protection via exorbitant operational cost (2^64 transfers = infeasible)

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: Transfers storage stores record but relies on Grandpa finality
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Transfer finality depends on block finality, not pallet-level guarantee
vulnerabilities.impact: Blockchain reorg could reverse settled transfers
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Acquire 2/3+1 Byzantine power, finalize competing fork
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - finality is consensus-layer responsibility
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: OUT_OF_SCOPE - Grandpa handled by consensus layer
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Relies on Grandpa BFT finality guarantee

vulnerabilities.description: Initiate but never settle transfer, harvest pending state
vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: Transfers kept in storage 'for audit; a later reaper can prune Finalized/Refunded/Failed'
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: No automatic cleanup - relies on future pruning mechanism not yet implemented
vulnerabilities.impact: Unbounded state growth if transfers not cleaned up
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Create many transfers, leave in SourceDebited state after expiry
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: FOUND - Comment indicates pruning 'can' happen but not implemented yet
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: NOT VISIBLE - no cleanup test
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Planned but not implemented pruning mechanism

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: BridgePaused storage map + 'BridgePaused' error variant
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Governance can set BridgePaused[chain_id] with reason, blocks xvm_transfer calls
vulnerabilities.impact: Initiated transfers can be locked if governance pauses before completion
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Governance votes to pause bridge after SourceDebited but before DestinationCredited
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - governance pause correctly blocks new transfers
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: IMPLIED - RootOrAny origin policy in tests allows governance simulation
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Pause mechanism requires governance approval

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: no emergency override visible for settled transfers
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Governance can pause but cannot forcibly settle or reverse settled transfers
vulnerabilities.impact: Governance power limited to pause, not override atomicity
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: N/A - settled transfers immutable by design
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - proper isolation of governance authority
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: IMPLIED - no override mechanism implemented
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Governance authority scoped to pause only

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: 'King invariant. Every ledger mutation is a single transactional call into the supply-ledger pallet'
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Supply ledger enforces conservation via transactional writes
vulnerabilities.impact: Supply cannot be inflated without breaking ledger invariant
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Create two parallel SourceDebited states for same asset → ledger rejects
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - ledger enforcement prevents duplication
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: COMPREHENSIVE - test_x3_native_evm_svm_roundtrip_preserves_supply explicitly checks supply conservation
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Transactional ledger writes enforce conservation invariant

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: cancel_expired_xvm_transfer drives 'SourceDebited → Expired → Refunded'
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: State machine enforces single refund per transfer via TransferStatus transitions
vulnerabilities.impact: Cannot refund same transfer twice
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Call cancel_expired_xvm_transfer twice → second call gets Refunded→Refunded illegal transition
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - state machine prevents double-refund
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: IMPLIED - state transition tests cover this
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: State machine enforces single transition path per transfer

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: xvm_transfer accepts sender: AccountBytes parameter directly
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Caller passes sender address; no signature validation at router layer
vulnerabilities.impact: Caller can claim to be any sender, consuming their nonce
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Call xvm_transfer claiming sender=alice_native but caller=bob
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: FOUND - Comment: 'Callable by a signed account that represents the sender on the source domain'
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: PARTIAL - no test showing sender validation
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Relies on VM-specific call validation (precompiles handle EVM/SVM sender verification)
vulnerabilities.fix_required: Add signature verification in xvm_transfer OR document that only precompiles can call safely

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: DomainId parameter disambiguates, but signature scope unclear
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Nonce is scoped to (source_domain, sender) but signature could be replayed across VMs
vulnerabilities.impact: Signature valid on X3Native might be replayed on X3Evm
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Intercept valid X3Native signature, replay on X3Evm with domain_id changed
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: PARTIAL - no visible domain-binding in signature structure
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: UNKNOWN - signature validation not shown in excerpt
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Nonce scoping provides partial protection, but full signature validation needed

vulnerabilities.description: Race condition between expiry check and completion
vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: cancel_expired_xvm_transfer checks 'NotYetExpired', but timing window exists
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Time checks at call time, but block production is non-deterministic
vulnerabilities.impact: Transfer might expire between `complete_xvm_transfer` submission and inclusion
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Submit complete_xvm_transfer at block N, node includes at block N+10 after expiry
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - architectural consequence of blockchain's asynchronous inclusion
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: NOT VISIBLE - timing-dependent race conditions hard to test
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Expiry blocks provide sufficient time window (expires_at usually 100+ blocks ≈ 20+ seconds)

vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: PendingCount storage + 'RoutePendingLimitExceeded' error
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Pending count checked on initiate but increased asynchronously
vulnerabilities.impact: Multiple parallel transactions could exceed pending limit
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Submit batch of transfers simultaneously → all see same pending_count before state update
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: NOT FOUND - transactional semantics prevent this
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: IMPLIED - txpool handles ordering
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Transactional state updates prevent concurrent count inflation

vulnerabilities.description: Different VMs disagree on transfer status
vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: Transfers stored locally per pallet instance, replicated across VMs
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: Each VM (X3Native, X3Evm, X3Svm) has independent pallet instance
vulnerabilities.impact: Alice sees transfer completed on X3Native but not yet on X3Evm
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Include block on X3Native before X3Evm, user sees inconsistent state
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: ARCHITECTURAL - cross-VM eventual consistency is inherent
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: PARTIAL - single test harness validates consistency
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Transactional proof-based settlement enforces consistency post-finality

vulnerabilities.description: VM state fork due to consensus disagreement
vulnerabilities.code_location: x3-cross-vm-router-lib.rs: relies on underlying Grandpa consensus
vulnerabilities.implementation_detail: If consensus forks, VMs fork independently
vulnerabilities.impact: Transfers could be finalized on one fork but not the other
vulnerabilities.exploit_path: Cause consensus fork with 1/3+1 Byzantine validators
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: OUT_OF_SCOPE - consensus-layer property
vulnerabilities.test_coverage: OUT_OF_SCOPE - consensus harness separate
vulnerabilities.mitigation_in_place: Relies on Grandpa BFT safety

critical_findings.title: Sender Address Forgery via Direct Parameter
critical_findings.description: xvm_transfer accepts sender: AccountBytes as parameter without verifying caller matches. A caller can claim to be any sender and consume their nonce allocation.
critical_findings.code_evidence: xvm_transfer(origin: OriginFor<T>, asset_id, source, destination, sender, recipient, amount, expires_at) - sender not validated against origin
critical_findings.impact: Attacker can consume target's nonce budget and initiate fraudulent transfers
critical_findings.recommended_fix: Add signature verification: require sender parameter matches origin, OR add sender validation via VM-specific precompiles OR document that only precompiles can safely call xvm_transfer

critical_findings.title: Unbounded Expiry Allows Indefinite Fund Lock
critical_findings.description: No upper bound on expires_at parameter. Attacker can set expires_at = u32::MAX (4 billion blocks ≈ 8000+ years), locking funds indefinitely.
critical_findings.code_evidence: xvm_transfer validates 'Expiry block is not in the future' but no upper bound check
critical_findings.impact: Funds locked for unreasonable duration, breaking liveness
critical_findings.recommended_fix: Add MaxExpiryBlocks parameter, validate expires_at <= current_block + MaxExpiryBlocks (recommend 10000 blocks ≈ 30 minutes)

critical_findings.title: Settled Transfers Never Pruned
critical_findings.description: Code comment says 'entries are retained after finalization for audit; a later reaper can prune'. Pruning mechanism not yet implemented. Unbounded state growth.
critical_findings.code_evidence: Transfers storage retention comment indicates planned but unimplemented pruning
critical_findings.impact: State bloat over time, potential DOS via storage exhaustion
critical_findings.recommended_fix: Implement on_initialize cleanup: prune Finalized/Refunded/Failed transfers older than PruneBlockAge (recommend 100000 blocks)

test_coverage_assessment.state_machine_testing: IMPLIED - state transitions covered in fixtures
test_coverage_assessment.expiry_handling_testing: PARTIAL - no upper bound test visible
test_coverage_assessment.multi_vm_consistency_testing: BASIC - single test harness validates consistency

reasoning: CRITICAL VULNERABILITY FOUND: Sender address forgery via unvalidated xvm_transfer parameter allows attackers to impersonate any user and consume their nonce allocation. This breaks authorization and enables griefing attacks. Additionally, unbounded expiry allows indefinite fund locking, and settled transfers never prune (unbounded state growth). While replay protection and atomic settlement mechanics are well-implemented and tested, the three vulnerabilities above must be fixed before mainnet launch.
required_fixes_before_mainnet: 1. CRITICAL: Add sender validation in xvm_transfer - verify parameter matches origin OR require VM-specific precompile calls only
2. HIGH: Add MaxExpiryBlocks parameter - cap expires_at to current_block + MaxExpiryBlocks (recommend 10000 blocks)
3. MEDIUM: Implement Transfers pruning - clean up Finalized/Refunded/Failed entries older than PruneBlockAge
